sub_sections.title: Objectives and ambition
sub_sections.content: MAESTRO targets eight measurable project objectives directly aligned with the expected outcomes of HORIZON-CL4-2026-05-DIGITAL-EMERGING-02: OBJ-1 (Neuro-symbolic planning engine for autonomous task decomposition, ATU/WP2) targets ≥40% improvement in plan success rate over LLM-based agent baselines on WebArena, SWE-bench, and GAIA for tasks exceeding 10 sequential steps; OBJ-2 (Unified adaptive memory architecture, BIIS/WP3) targets ≥30% improvement in task coherence over context-window-only baselines in interactions exceeding 50 agent steps; OBJ-3 (Decentralised multi-agent coordination protocols, CERIA/WP4) targets ≥25% improvement in joint task completion over independent baselines with formal convergence proofs for ≥5 heterogeneous agents; and OBJ-8 (External Tool and API Orchestration Layer, ATU) targets a typed tool registry supporting ≥30 external tools/APIs with ≥95% invocation success rate. These objectives are measurable against standardised benchmarks and verifiable through clinical expert validation at NIHS (OBJ-4) and operational factory trials at ISIA (OBJ-5), making them credible and realistically achievable within 48 months by a consortium that unites foundational AI research expertise (ATU, CERIA, BIIS) with sector-leading industry deployment experience (NIHS, ISIA, ELI). MAESTRO advances substantially beyond the state of the art on three fronts: first, current LLM-based agents rely on chain-of-thought prompting that degrades unpredictably over long task horizons — MAESTRO's neuro-symbolic planning engine introduces formally specified sub-task graphs with pre/post-conditions, Monte Carlo Tree Search and hierarchical task network algorithms, and verified plan self-correction, providing formal guarantees absent from pure LLM-based approaches; second, working memory, episodic memory, and semantic memory are co-designed into a unified tri-store architecture with a learned memory controller — the first integrated system of this scope to manage information commit, retrieval, and forgetting across all three store types; third, multi-agent coordination protocols with provable convergence and auditable delegation traces close a critical accountability gap in current ad-hoc LLM-based agent communication frameworks. MAESTRO positions its R&I from TRL 2 at project start (foundational architectures developed from first principles in WP2–WP4) to TRL 5 at project end (validated in operational environments: clinical decision support at NIHS/WP5, process optimisation at ISIA/WP6, and logistics operations at ELI/WP7 by month 42), consistent with RIA maturity expectations.

sub_sections.title: Methodology
sub_sections.content: MAESTRO's methodology is founded on the architectural co-design of three mutually reinforcing research pillars and a cross-cutting External Tool and API Orchestration Layer, with the core methodological assumption that AI agent robustness and real-world deployability require simultaneous integration of structured planning, persistent memory, and multi-agent coordination — not independent modules composed ex post. WP2 (Neuro-Symbolic Planning and Reasoning Engine, ATU lead) combines LLM-based heuristic evaluation with symbolic search algorithms (MCTS and HTN planning) to produce executable sub-task graphs with formally specified pre/post-conditions and a plan monitoring module enabling verified self-correction from intermediate states, with architecture specification D2-01 (M18) and planning engine software prototype D2-02 (M36). WP3 (Adaptive Memory Architecture, BIIS lead) implements a tri-store design — working memory (structured short-term agent state), episodic memory (retrieval-augmented experience store indexed by context similarity), and semantic memory (persistent knowledge graph of domain concepts and tool capabilities) — governed by a learned memory controller balancing recall accuracy against computational cost, with adaptive memory system software D3-02 (M30). WP4 (Decentralised Multi-Agent Coordination, CERIA lead) develops formal task-delegation and negotiation protocols with provable convergence, conflict resolution under partial observability, and auditable structured interaction traces, delivering decentralised coordination software D4-02 (M36). WP8 (Integration, Evaluation and Benchmarking, FIIT lead) integrates all pillars into the unified MAESTRO framework and releases a standardised community benchmark suite D8-02 (M42). Interdisciplinary integration is intrinsic: WP2 bridges LLM-based NLP and formal symbolic AI planning; WP3 combines neural retrieval with knowledge graph reasoning; WP4 applies game-theoretic multi-agent systems and formal protocol verification; sector demonstrators require depth in clinical informatics (WP5, NIHS), industrial engineering (WP6, ISIA), and supply chain operations (WP7, ELI). This project is primarily technical, focused on AI agent architectures and computational system design; no specific SSH integration is required for this work programme topic, and no gender dimension in R&I content is applicable to the algorithm and system design work in WP2–WP4 or the demonstrator evaluations in WP5–WP7. Open science practices include full open-source release of the MAESTRO framework (D9-02, WP9, BAL lead, M36), contribution of all models, benchmarks, and datasets to the AI-on-demand platform per SR-10, and a data management plan (D1-02, WP1, ATU lead, M6) ensuring FAIR data principles govern all research data produced.

claim_statuses.claim_summary: OBJ-1 target: ≥40% plan success rate improvement over LLM baselines on WebArena, SWE-bench, GAIA
OBJ-2 target: ≥30% task coherence improvement over context-window-only baselines
OBJ-3 target: ≥25% joint task completion improvement, formal convergence, ≥5 agents
OBJ-8 target: ≥30 tools/APIs, ≥95% invocation success, full provenance traces
TRL 2 at project start (foundational architectures); TRL 5 at project end (operational environments)
Neuro-symbolic planning: MCTS, HTN algorithms, formally specified pre/post-conditions, plan self-correction
Tri-store memory architecture (working, episodic, semantic) with learned memory controller
Formally specified multi-agent delegation protocols with provable convergence and auditable traces
External Tool and API Orchestration Layer as cross-cutting architectural element spanning all pillars
Open-source framework release D9-02 (WP9, BAL lead, M36)
Data management plan D1-02 (WP1, ATU lead, M6) ensuring FAIR data principles
AI-on-demand platform contribution of models, benchmarks, datasets per SR-10
